title: Chicken & Shrimp Miso Skewers with Sesame Soba Noodles and Cucumber-Radish Slaw
description: Umami-forward miso-marinated chicken and shrimp skewers served over sesame soba noodles with a crisp cucumber-radish slaw. Components can be prepped ahead — slaw and noodles hold well.

ingredients.name: boneless skinless chicken breast, cut into 1.5-inch cubes
large shrimp (16-20 count), peeled and deveined
low-sodium soy sauce or tamari (for noodles)
English cucumber, thinly sliced into half-moons
nori sheet, cut into thin strips (optional)

instructions.text: Make the miso marinade: whisk together miso paste, soy sauce, rice vinegar, ginger, garlic, and avocado oil until smooth. Divide roughly 60/40 — the larger portion for chicken, smaller for shrimp. Add chicken to the larger portion, toss to coat, cover and refrigerate.

Make the slaw: combine cucumber and radishes in a bowl. Toss with rice vinegar, sesame oil, salt, and pepper. Refrigerate — it improves as it sits. Add nori strips on top just before serving if using.
Add shrimp to the smaller marinade portion and toss to coat. Do not marinate longer than 20 minutes — the acid will start to denature the shrimp.

Cook soba noodles per package instructions, typically 4–5 minutes — don't overcook, they go mushy. Drain and rinse immediately under cold water to stop cooking. Toss with soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and half the sesame seeds and scallions. Set aside at room temperature.
Thread chicken and shrimp on separate skewers — they cook at different rates. Preheat grill to medium-high. Wipe grates clean and oil lightly with avocado oil — miso sugars char fast.

Grill chicken skewers 4–5 minutes per side until cooked through (165°F internal). Add shrimp skewers when you flip the chicken — shrimp take only 2–3 minutes total, flipping once. They're done when pink and just curled. Pull them fast — overcooked shrimp is the main failure mode here.
Serve skewers over soba noodles with slaw alongside. Garnish with remaining sesame seeds and scallions.

notes:
Soba note: buy 100% buckwheat soba — many brands cut it with wheat flour. Check the label. True buckwheat soba is gluten-free and has a lower glycemic impact.

Grill tip: medium-high heat, not high. The miso sugars will char quickly on high heat.

Make-ahead: slaw can be made the morning of. Chicken can marinate all day. Noodles hold well at room temperature dressed for 1–2 hours.

Swap: salmon or mackerel can replace the shrimp for a bigger omega-3 hit.
